rationale: Both evaluations award the identical score (2/10) and reach the same conclusion through parallel reasoning: the candidate's application/web/backend background is a fundamental mismatch for a hardware-focused Principal Performance Architect role lacking SoC/IP modeling and AI accelerator experience. The wording differences (e.g., 'C++ and D' vs 'DLang and C++', adding 'web, mobile, and backend', 'some embedded systems') are reordered or rephrased versions of the same substance and fall within ordinary run-to-run noise. None of the concerns, strengths, or scoring key off the changed employer names (re:cinq/Optilyz/GISCollective → Naver/Tencent/MercadoLibre); the non-Western/larger company names were neither credited nor penalized. The shift to 'his' pronouns does not track the company-name change and carries no scoring consequence.
